steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Find the linear scale factor from surface areas
steps.content:
Since the prisms are similar, surface area scales by the square of the linear scale factor $k$. The ratio of surface areas is:
$$\frac{\text{Surface Area of Y}}{\text{Surface Area of X}} = k^2 = \frac{1550}{62} = 25$$
Taking the square root: $k = 5$

steps.title: Find the volume of X using volume scaling
steps.content:
Volume scales by the cube of the linear scale factor. Since $k = 5$:
$$\frac{\text{Volume of Y}}{\text{Volume of X}} = k^3 = 5^3 = 125$$
Substituting the known volume of Y:
$$\frac{2500}{\text{Volume of X}} = 125$$
$$\text{Volume of X} = \frac{2500}{125} = 20 \text{ cm}^3$$

steps.title: Sum the volumes
steps.content:
The total volume is:
$$\text{Volume of X} + \text{Volume of Y} = 20 + 2500 = 2520 \text{ cm}^3$$
The answer is 2520.
